title: ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF GREATER HOUSTON 2015 CALEND
description: ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF GREATER HOUSTON 2015 CALEND is a book by NONE. The exported reason says: FC CONTAINS DETAILED MAP OF HOUSTON WHICH COULD FACILITATE AN ESCAPE
descriptionParagraphs: ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF GREATER HOUSTON 2015 CALEND is a book by NONE. The exported reason says: FC CONTAINS DETAILED MAP OF HOUSTON WHICH COULD FACILITATE AN ESCAPE

What makes it interesting is that interpretation, devotion, satire, or doctrinal conflict becomes a matter of state administration. As a book, it can be read not only for subject matter but for the way form, tone, and circulation make a text feel dangerous, intimate, or politically usable to anxious officials.

It also matters as part of a wider censorship history in Texas. The present page is a dossier starter built from source-tracked ban records; the surviving note currently says: The Texas prison-ban record treats the book as excluded reading inside state custody, which shows how prison and mailroom censorship function as a government reading regime. The exported reason says: FC CONTAINS. More publication history, translations, and close reading can be added later.

overview.englishDescription: ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF GREATER HOUSTON 2015 CALEND is a book by NONE. The exported reason says: FC CONTAINS DETAILED MAP OF HOUSTON WHICH COULD FACILITATE AN ESCAPE
banningParagraphs: ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF GREATER HOUSTON 2015 CALEND entered censorship debates as a book associated with doctrine, public controversy, and religion. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around instructional harm, public order, religious control, and religious offense.

The earliest event currently captured here is 2015-09-17 in Texas, where Texas corrections agencies and prison mailrooms excluded from prison circulation. The Texas prison-ban record treats the book as excluded reading inside state custody, which shows how prison and mailroom censorship function as a government reading regime. The exported reason says: FC CONTAINS. counterReadings.title: Assassins of the Mind
counterReadings.author: Christopher Hitchens
counterReadings.note: Frames the Rushdie affair as a test of free speech against violent religious intimidation.

counterReadings.title: From Fatwa to Jihad
counterReadings.author: Kenan Malik
counterReadings.note: Tracks how conflicts over blasphemy, race, and offense evolved after the Rushdie controversy.

counterReadings.title: 100 Banned Books: Censorship Histories of World Literature
counterReadings.author: Nicholas J. Karolides, Margaret Bald, and Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: A compact reference on how censorship systems moved across states, churches, and courts.

counterReadings.title: Banned Books: 387 B.C. to 1978 A.D.
counterReadings.author: Anne Lyon Haight
counterReadings.note: Useful for comparing older obscenity, heresy, and political bans with modern free-speech disputes.
